verdict_reasoning: STIM1 is primarily an ER-resident type I transmembrane protein. However, a documented minor fraction does traffic to the plasma membrane under specific conditions (Ca2+ store depletion, certain cell types like pancreatic cancer cells) and exhibits dual topology at the PM. Recent literature (2026) confirms cell-surface expression of STIM1 in some cell types and conditions, though most functional work highlights ER-localized signaling. The protein possesses a single transmembrane domain and N-linked glycosylation motifs that support surface trafficking. While the dominant pool is intracellular (ER), the documented surface fraction under defined conditions meets the threshold for contextual surface accessibility.
